{"id":"project-LICHEN-oue2","title":"crash_info: potential false positive on first power-on boot","description":"File: lichen/subsys/lichen/l2/crash_info.c
Lines: 52-70 (crash_info_check_and_clear function)

On the very first power-on boot (not warm reset), the __noinit crash_info structure contains uninitialized garbage values. There is a 1-in-2^32 probability that this garbage could match CRASH_MAGIC (0xDEAD0001), causing a false 'previous crash' log message with garbage data.

Current behavior:
1. First boot: __noinit RAM = garbage
2. crash_info_check_and_clear() checks if crash_info.magic == CRASH_MAGIC
3. If garbage matches (1 in ~4 billion), logs a false crash warning with garbage reason/location/extra

While the probability is extremely low, it could cause confusion during initial deployment debugging.

Potential fix options:
1. Accept the 1-in-2^32 risk (current behavior, probably acceptable)
2. Add a secondary magic/checksum over the crash_info fields
3. Use a pattern like 0xDEADxxxx where xxxx is a CRC of the other fields

Severity: P4 (Low) - The probability is negligible for most deployments, but documenting this edge case is valuable.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:47:16.241426Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:47:16.241729Z"}

2. Add a secondary magic/checksum over the crash_info fields
3. Use a pattern like 0xDEADxxxx where xxxx is a CRC of the other fields

Severity: P4 (Low) - The probability is negligible for most deployments, but documenting this edge case is valuable.","status":"in_progress","priority":4,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"AI Agent","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:47:16.241426Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-23T04:22:23.93163563Z","started_at":"2026-07-23T04:22:23.93163563Z","lease_expires_at":"2026-07-23T04:27:23.93163563Z","heartbeat_at":"2026-07-23T04:22:23.93163563Z"}
new_value: {"notes":"Implementing dual-magic validation to eliminate false positive on first boot (1-in-2^64 probability instead of 1-in-2^32)"}
